title: Java Sequenced Collections
description: Read both ends and reverse a collection with the sequenced collections API.
learningGoals: Access the first and last elements with getFirst and getLast (Java 21).
Recall that reversed() gives a reverse-order view without index arithmetic.

completionChecks.prompt: What does q.getFirst() return after q.addFirst("start")?
completionChecks.explanation: addFirst inserts at the front of the sequenced collection, so getFirst reads the inserted value.

completionChecks.prompt: What is the purpose of q.reversed()?
choices: To view the same sequence in reverse order
completionChecks.explanation: reversed() exposes the collection in reverse encounter order; it is not a sorting or removal operation.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the println statement print after addFirst("start")?
completionChecks.explanation: addFirst changes the first element, while reversed() views the encounter order backward without sorting the values.
